meaning_en: A small group within a larger group
example: Religious minorities have equal rights under the law.
mnemonic: MINORITY — MINOR + ITY; the MINOR (smaller) part = minority.

meaning_en: An important task or purpose assigned to someone
example: Their mission was to bring clean water to remote villages.
mnemonic: MISSION — MISSILE; a MISSILE has one MISSION — to hit its target.

meaning_en: Relating to right and wrong behaviour; a lesson from a story
example: The story has a clear moral: honesty always wins.
mnemonic: MORAL — MORE + AL; MORE goodness in ALL actions = moral.

meaning_en: To provide someone with a reason or desire to act
example: Good teachers motivate students to learn more.
mnemonic: MOTIVATE — MOTIVE + ATE; a strong motive motivates you.

meaning_en: Difficult to explain or understand; full of mystery
example: A mysterious figure appeared at the door.
mnemonic: MYSTERIOUS — MYSTERY + OUS; full of mystery = mysterious.

meaning_en: To discuss and reach an agreement
example: The union negotiated a pay rise for the workers.
mnemonic: NEGOTIATE — NEGOTI + ATE; eat (ATE) negotiations for breakfast to get a deal.

meaning_en: Not taking sides in a conflict; having no strong features
example: Switzerland remained neutral during the war.
mnemonic: NEUTRAL — NEUT + RAL; neither this nor that — neutral.

meaning_en: Despite that; in spite of what has been said
example: It was raining; nevertheless, they continued the match.
mnemonic: NEVERTHELESS — NEVER + THE + LESS; never the less important point.

meaning_en: The process of getting the right food for health
example: Good nutrition is essential for children's development.
mnemonic: NUTRITION — NUTRIENT + ION; the process of taking in nutrients.

meaning_en: A goal or target; not influenced by personal feelings
example: Our main objective is to reduce carbon emissions.
mnemonic: OBJECTIVE — OBJECT + IVE; an object you aim for = objective.

meaning_en: The act of carefully watching and noting something
example: Through careful observation, scientists made the discovery.
mnemonic: OBSERVATION — OBSERVE + ATION; the result of observing.

meaning_en: Something that blocks progress or success
example: Fear is often the biggest obstacle to success.
mnemonic: OBSTACLE — OB + STACLE; a STABLE block in your path = obstacle.

meaning_en: Hoping and expecting things to turn out well
example: She remains optimistic despite the challenges ahead.
mnemonic: OPTIMISTIC — OPTIC + MIST; seeing through the MIST with OPTImism.

meaning_en: The result or effect of an action or event
example: The outcome of the election surprised everyone.
mnemonic: OUTCOME — OUT + COME; what COMES OUT at the end = outcome.

meaning_en: To succeed in dealing with a difficult problem
example: She overcame her fear of public speaking.
mnemonic: OVERCOME — OVER + COME; COME OVER the challenge = overcome.

meaning_en: In or to a foreign country across the sea
example: She got a job offer overseas and decided to move.
mnemonic: OVERSEAS — OVER + SEAS; travel OVER the SEAS to another country.

meaning_en: Taking part in an activity or event
example: Student participation in class improves learning.
mnemonic: PARTICIPATION — PART + ICIPATION; playing your PART = participation.

meaning_en: A relationship in which people work together
example: The project was completed through a strong partnership.
mnemonic: PARTNERSHIP — PARTNER + SHIP; sharing a SHIP = partnership.

meaning_en: Having very strong feelings or enthusiasm about something
example: She is passionate about environmental protection.
mnemonic: PASSIONATE — PASSION + ATE; someone who ate (consumed) by passion.

meaning_en: The ability to wait calmly without becoming annoyed
example: Patience is a virtue — great things take time.
mnemonic: PATIENCE — PATIENT + CE; a patient person has great patience.

meaning_en: A punishment for breaking a rule or law
example: There is a heavy penalty for littering in Singapore.
mnemonic: PENALTY — PEN + ALTY; put the PEN on paper and sign the PENALTY.

meaning_en: The way you understand or see something
example: Perception of time changes when you're busy.
mnemonic: PERCEPTION — PERCEIVE + TION; how you perceive = your perception.

meaning_en: A particular way of thinking about something; a viewpoint
example: Travel gives you a wider perspective on life.
mnemonic: PERSPECTIVE — PER + SPECTIVE; looking THROUGH (per) a SPEC(tacle).

meaning_en: A remarkable or extraordinary fact or event
example: The Northern Lights are a stunning natural phenomenon.
mnemonic: PHENOMENON — PHENOM + ENON; a PHENOM(enal) event = phenomenon.

meaning_en: The study of fundamental questions about existence and knowledge
example: Her philosophy in life is to always be kind.
mnemonic: PHILOSOPHY — PHILO + SOPHY; philo = love, sophy = wisdom; love of wisdom.

meaning_en: Having the possibility to develop into something in the future
example: Every student has the potential to succeed.
mnemonic: POTENTIAL — POTENT + IAL; being POTENT (powerful) = having potential.

meaning_en: Exact and accurate in every detail
example: Be precise when describing symptoms to the doctor.
mnemonic: PRECISE — PRE + CISE; cut (incise) beforehand with total accuracy.

meaning_en: The most important thing that needs attention first
example: Health should always be your top priority.
mnemonic: PRIORITY — PRIOR + ITY; what comes PRIOR (first) = priority.

meaning_en: A series of steps followed to complete a task
example: Follow the correct procedure when applying for a visa.
mnemonic: PROCEDURE — PRO + CEED + URE; proceed step by step = procedure.

meaning_en: A part or share in relation to a whole
example: A large proportion of the population uses smartphones.
mnemonic: PROPORTION — PRO + PORTION; each PORTION proportioned correctly.

meaning_en: The possibility of something happening in the future
example: The prospect of a new job excited her.
mnemonic: PROSPECT — PRO + SPECT; look ahead (pro = forward) to the future.

meaning_en: Relating to the mind and mental processes
example: The accident had a serious psychological impact on her.
mnemonic: PSYCHOLOGICAL — PSYCHO + LOGICAL; the logic of the mind.

meaning_en: The act of publishing; a published book or magazine
example: Her first publication was a short story collection.
mnemonic: PUBLICATION — PUBLIC + ATION; making something PUBLIC = publication.

meaning_en: To follow or strive to achieve something
meaning_hi: पीछे करना / लक्ष्य की ओर बढ़ना
example: She decided to pursue a career in journalism.
mnemonic: PURSUE — PURR + SUE; a cat PURRsues a mouse = pursue.

meaning_en: Prejudice or discrimination based on race
example: Racism has no place in a fair and equal society.
mnemonic: RACISM — RACE + ISM; an -ism (belief system) based on RACE.

meaning_en: Happening in a short time; very fast
example: There has been rapid growth in the technology sector.
mnemonic: RAPID — RAP + ID; a RAP ID(entification) is made rapidly.

meaning_en: Fair and sensible; not extreme
example: She made a reasonable request that everyone agreed with.
mnemonic: REASONABLE — REASON + ABLE; able to reason = reasonable.

meaning_en: A period of economic decline
example: Many businesses shut down during the recession.
mnemonic: RECESSION — RE + CESSION; a CESSION (retreat) of economic activity.

meaning_en: A return to a normal state after illness or difficulty
example: Her recovery from surgery was faster than expected.
mnemonic: RECOVERY — RE + COVER + Y; RE-COVER yourself = recovery.

meaning_en: A person forced to leave their country due to danger
example: Thousands of refugees fled the war-torn country.
mnemonic: REFUGEE — REFUge + GEE; someone seeking REFUGE = refugee.

meaning_en: To control something by rules or laws
example: The government regulates food safety standards.
mnemonic: REGULATE — REGUL + ATE; a rule ATE (consumed) all exceptions = regulate.

meaning_en: Closely connected and important to the current situation
example: Make sure your examples are relevant to the topic.
mnemonic: RELEVANT — RELEV + ANT; an ANT always finds the RELEVant food.

meaning_en: Extraordinary or worthy of attention
example: She made a remarkable recovery from her illness.
mnemonic: REMARKABLE — REMARK + ABLE; worthy of being remarked upon.

meaning_en: The general opinion people have about someone or something
example: The restaurant has an excellent reputation for food.
mnemonic: REPUTATION — REPUTE + ATION; what others repute (say) about you.

meaning_en: A firm decision; the solution to a problem
example: Her New Year's resolution was to exercise daily.
mnemonic: RESOLUTION — RE + SOLUTION; finding a SOLUTION again = resolution.

meaning_en: To return something to its original state
example: The old painting was carefully restored to its former glory.
mnemonic: RESTORE — RE + STORE; put back in STORE (original state).

meaning_en: To put a limit on something
example: Speed limits restrict how fast you can drive.
mnemonic: RESTRICT — RE + STRICT; STRICT rules RESTRICT behaviour.

meaning_en: A dramatic change in systems or ideas; a full circular movement
example: The Industrial Revolution changed the world forever.
mnemonic: REVOLUTION — REVOLVE + TION; revolving to create change = revolution.

meaning_en: Something given in recognition of achievement or service
example: Hard work brings its own rewards.
mnemonic: REWARD — RE + WARD; WARD off trouble and get a REWARD.

meaning_en: Relating to the countryside, not the city
example: Healthcare access in rural areas needs improvement.
mnemonic: RURAL — MURAL; a MURAL painted on countryside walls = rural.

meaning_en: A device orbiting the earth; a smaller thing depending on a larger one
example: GPS works through a network of satellites.
mnemonic: SATELLITE — SATEL + LITE; a LITE (light) in the sky = satellite.

meaning_en: An action or event regarded as morally wrong
example: The politician resigned after the financial scandal.
mnemonic: SCANDAL — SCAN + DAL; SCAN the DAL (problem) = scandal revealed.

meaning_en: A person who has deep knowledge in a subject
example: She is a renowned scholar of ancient Indian history.
mnemonic: SCHOLAR — SCHOOL + AR; a SCHOOL master = scholar.

meaning_en: A distinct part of an economy, society, or area
example: The technology sector has grown rapidly in the last decade.
mnemonic: SECTOR — SECT + OR; a SECT (section) of the bigger area.

meaning_en: Quick to detect or notice things; easily upset
example: She is very sensitive to criticism.
mnemonic: SENSITIVE — SENSE + ITIVE; highly attuned SENSES = sensitive.

meaning_en: Very serious or extreme in degree
example: The region experienced severe flooding this year.
mnemonic: SEVERE — SE + VERE; SEVEREly cut = severe impact.

meaning_en: Important or notable; having meaning
example: There has been a significant improvement in air quality.
mnemonic: SIGNIFICANT — SIGN + IFICANT; leaving a SIGN = significant.

meaning_en: Highly developed and complex; cultured and refined
example: The new software uses sophisticated artificial intelligence.
mnemonic: SOPHISTICATED — SOPHIST + ICATED; a SOPHIST (wise debater) = sophisticated.

meaning_en: The origin or beginning of something
example: The river is the main source of water for the village.
mnemonic: SOURCE — COURSE; the SOURCE sets the COURSE of a river.

meaning_en: A group of living organisms sharing characteristics
example: Many species of animals are endangered today.
mnemonic: SPECIES — SPICE + S; each SPICE is a different SPECIES of flavour.

meaning_en: Beautiful or impressive in a dramatic way
example: The fireworks display was absolutely spectacular.
mnemonic: SPECTACULAR — SPECTACLE + AR; a great SPECTACLE is spectacular.

meaning_en: A person or organisation that provides funds for an event
example: A local company sponsored the school sports event.
mnemonic: SPONSOR — SPONS + OR; a sponsor responds (spons) with money.

meaning_en: Not likely to change or fail; firmly established
example: The patient's condition is now stable.
mnemonic: STABLE — a STABLE building doesn't shake in storms.

meaning_en: Numerical data collected and analysed
example: Statistics show that life expectancy is increasing.
mnemonic: STATISTICS — STAT + ISTICS; STATIC numbers = statistics.

meaning_en: The social or professional standing of a person or thing
example: Her status in the company rose after the project's success.
mnemonic: STATUS — STAT + US; the STAT(e) of US in society = status.

meaning_en: Demanding that rules are followed exactly
example: The school has strict rules about uniform.
mnemonic: STRICT — RESTRICT; something strict restricts freedom.

meaning_en: To try hard to do something difficult; a hard effort
example: Many people struggle to balance work and family life.
mnemonic: STRUGGLE — STRUG + GLE; strung with effort = struggle.

meaning_en: To hand in work or a document for consideration
example: Please submit your assignment before the deadline.
mnemonic: SUBMIT — SUB + MIT; put it UNDER (sub) the MIT (authority).

meaning_en: Enough for a particular purpose
example: Is there sufficient evidence to prove the case?
mnemonic: SUFFICIENT — SUFFICE + IENT; to SUFFICE means to be enough = sufficient.

meaning_en: Able to be maintained without harming the environment
example: We need sustainable energy sources like solar and wind.
mnemonic: SUSTAINABLE — SUSTAIN + ABLE; ABLE to SUSTAIN over time.

meaning_en: A mark or character used to represent something
example: The dove is a symbol of peace.
mnemonic: SYMBOL — SYM + BOL; a BOWL (bol) shape can be a SYMbol.

meaning_en: Lasting for only a limited time
example: The road is closed; it's a temporary arrangement.
mnemonic: TEMPORARY — TEMPO + RARY; a TEMPO (beat) that is RARE = temporary.

meaning_en: Mental or emotional stress; stretched tightness
example: There was growing tension between the two teams.
mnemonic: TENSION — TENT + ION; a TENT pulled tight = tension.

meaning_en: The use of violence to create fear for political purposes
example: Terrorism is a global threat that affects every nation.
mnemonic: TERRORISM — TERROR + ISM; an ideology (ism) of TERROR.

meaning_en: A set of ideas that explain something; a supposition
example: Einstein's theory of relativity changed physics.
mnemonic: THEORY — THOR + Y; THOR's WHY = a theory about lightning.

meaning_en: A statement of intention to harm; a possible danger
example: Climate change is the biggest threat to our planet.
mnemonic: THREAT — THREE + AT; three threats AT once = dangerous.

meaning_en: A custom passed down through generations
example: Every culture has its own unique traditions.
mnemonic: TRADITION — TRAD + ITION; old TRADe routes carried TRADITIONS.

meaning_en: To change completely in form, appearance, or character
example: Technology has transformed how we communicate.
mnemonic: TRANSFORM — TRANS + FORM; change FORM across (TRANS) = transform.

meaning_en: The process of changing from one state to another
example: The transition from school to university can be challenging.
mnemonic: TRANSITION — TRANSIT + ION; in TRANSIT from one state to another.

meaning_en: The best or most extreme example of something; final
example: Winning the World Cup was the ultimate achievement.
mnemonic: ULTIMATE — ULTIMA + TE; the last (ultima) level = ultimate.

meaning_en: Relating to a city or town
example: Urban areas face problems like pollution and traffic.
mnemonic: URBAN — URB + AN; an URBan AN(imal) lives in the city.

meaning_en: Absolutely necessary or important for life
example: Water is vital for human survival.
mnemonic: VITAL — VITA (life in Latin); vital = life-giving.

meaning_en: Done willingly, without being forced or paid
example: She does voluntary work at a shelter every weekend.
mnemonic: VOLUNTARY — VOLUNT + ARY; what you do of your own WILL (voluntas).

meaning_en: A regular payment for work, usually paid weekly
example: The minimum wage was increased this year.
mnemonic: WAGE — CAGE; you're trapped in a CAGE if your WAGE is too low.

meaning_en: A large amount of money or valuable possessions
example: True wealth is not just money but good health.
mnemonic: WEALTH — HEALTH; WEALTH without HEALTH is nothing.

meaning_en: The health, happiness, and well-being of a person or group
example: The government promotes the welfare of senior citizens.
mnemonic: WELFARE — WELL + FARE; fare WELL = welfare.

meaning_en: Distributed over a large area or existing in many places
example: Widespread flooding affected thousands of homes.
mnemonic: WIDESPREAD — WIDE + SPREAD; spread WIDE across a region.

meaning_en: Wild animals and plants living in their natural habitat
example: The national park protects diverse wildlife.
mnemonic: WILDLIFE — WILD + LIFE; life in the WILD = wildlife.

meaning_en: The ability to make good decisions from experience
example: With age comes wisdom and a better understanding of life.
mnemonic: WISDOM — WISE + DOM; the DOMAIN of the WISE = wisdom.

meaning_en: To take money out; to remove yourself from a situation
example: She withdrew cash from the ATM.
mnemonic: WITHDRAW — WITH + DRAW; draw money WITH you = withdraw.

meaning_en: A person who sees an event; to see something happen
example: She was a key witness in the trial.
mnemonic: WITNESS — WIT + NESS; a WIT(ty) presence = sharp witness.

meaning_en: The people employed in an organisation or economy
example: Technology is changing the workforce rapidly.
mnemonic: WORKFORCE — WORK + FORCE; the FORCE of WORKERS in an economy.

meaning_en: A session for learning a skill; a room where things are made
example: She attended a writing workshop over the weekend.
mnemonic: WORKSHOP — WORK + SHOP; a SHOP where you WORK and learn.

meaning_en: Existing or happening throughout the world
example: The song became a worldwide hit overnight.
mnemonic: WORLDWIDE — WORLD + WIDE; spread WIDE across the WORLD.

meaning_en: An area set aside for a particular purpose
example: This is a no-parking zone.
mnemonic: ZONE — PHONE; different PHONE codes for different ZONES.
